content.text:
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:6Z97IX | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Features
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:6Z97IX | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Quick Start
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:6Z97IX | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:6Z97IX | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | The script will:
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:6Z97IX | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Run the application
| MCP:6Z97IX | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | docker compose up -d
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Project Structure
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:6Z97IX | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:6Z97IX | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:6Z97IX | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── config/
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:6Z97IX | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:6Z97IX | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:6Z97IX | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## API Usage
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:6Z97IX | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:6Z97IX | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:6Z97IX | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:6Z97IX | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:6Z97IX | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```java
| MCP:6Z97IX | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:6Z97IX | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:6Z97IX | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:6Z97IX | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Idempotency
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```java
| MCP:6Z97IX | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:6Z97IX | @Idempotent
| MCP:6Z97IX | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:6Z97IX | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:6Z97IX | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```sh
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Run test environment
| MCP:6Z97IX | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Run prod environment
| MCP:6Z97IX | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:6Z97IX | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:6Z97IX | ```
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## Contributing
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup instructions, code style, and PR process.
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ---
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | ## License
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

| MCP:6Z97IX | #!/usr/bin/env bash
| MCP:6Z97IX | set -euo pipefail
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # =============================================================================
| MCP:6Z97IX | # init-project.sh — Initialize a new project from SpringBootTemplate
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Usage: ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact myapp --name "My App" --author "John Doe"
| MCP:6Z97IX | # =============================================================================
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Color helpers ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | RED='\033[0;31m'
| MCP:6Z97IX | GREEN='\033[0;32m'
| MCP:6Z97IX | YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
| MCP:6Z97IX | CYAN='\033[0;36m'
| MCP:6Z97IX | NC='\033[0m'
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | info()  { printf "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC}  %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok()    { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${NC}    %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:6Z97IX | warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC}  %s\n" "$1"; }
| MCP:6Z97IX | error() { printf "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} %s\n" "$1" >&2; }
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Detect OS for sed -i compatibility ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     SED_I=(sed -i '')
| MCP:6Z97IX | else
| MCP:6Z97IX |     SED_I=(sed -i)
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Parse arguments ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | GROUP_ID=""
| MCP:6Z97IX | ARTIFACT=""
| MCP:6Z97IX | NAME=""
| MCP:6Z97IX | AUTHOR=""
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
| MCP:6Z97IX |     case $1 in
| MCP:6Z97IX |         --group-id) GROUP_ID="$2"; shift 2 ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |         --artifact) ARTIFACT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |         --name)     NAME="$2";     shift 2 ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |         --author)   AUTHOR="$2";   shift 2 ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |         -h|--help)
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "Usage: ./init-project.sh --group-id <groupId> --artifact <artifact> [--name <name>] [--author <author>]"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "Options:"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "  --group-id   Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. com.acme)       [required]"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "  --artifact   Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. myapp, demo-app)       [required]"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "  --name       Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact)"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             echo "  --author     Author name for @author tags (default: keeps 'tzesh')"
| MCP:6Z97IX |             exit 0
| MCP:6Z97IX |             ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |         *) error "Unknown option: $1"; exit 1 ;;
| MCP:6Z97IX |     esac
| MCP:6Z97IX | done
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Interactive prompts for missing required values ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -z "$GROUP_ID" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     read -rp "Enter groupId (e.g. com.acme): " GROUP_ID
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -z "$ARTIFACT" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     read -rp "Enter artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. myapp): " ARTIFACT
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Validate inputs ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -z "$GROUP_ID" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     error "group-id is required"; exit 1
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -z "$ARTIFACT" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     error "artifact is required"; exit 1
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | if ! echo "$GROUP_ID" | grep -qE '^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9]*)+$'; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     error "Invalid group-id format: '$GROUP_ID' (expected e.g. com.acme)"; exit 1
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | if ! echo "$ARTIFACT" | grep -qE '^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(-[a-z][a-z0-9]*)*$'; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     error "Invalid artifact format: '$ARTIFACT' (expected lowercase kebab-case, e.g. myapp or my-app)"; exit 1
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Idempotency check ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ ! -d "spring-boot-template-core" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     error "Directory 'spring-boot-template-core' not found. Project may already be initialized."
| MCP:6Z97IX |     exit 1
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Derive values ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | ARTIFACT_NO_HYPHENS="${ARTIFACT//-/}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | PACKAGE_NAME="${GROUP_ID}.${ARTIFACT_NO_HYPHENS}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | PACKAGE_PATH="${PACKAGE_NAME//.//}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | ARTIFACT_LOWER="${ARTIFACT//-/_}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # PascalCase: split on hyphens, capitalize each word
| MCP:6Z97IX | ARTIFACT_PASCAL=""
| MCP:6Z97IX | IFS='-' read -ra PARTS <<< "$ARTIFACT"
| MCP:6Z97IX | for part in "${PARTS[@]}"; do
| MCP:6Z97IX |     ARTIFACT_PASCAL+="$(tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' <<< "${part:0:1}")${part:1}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | done
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | CORE_MODULE="${ARTIFACT}-core"
| MCP:6Z97IX | API_MODULE="${ARTIFACT}-api"
| MCP:6Z97IX | DB_NAME_PREFIX="${ARTIFACT_LOWER}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | CONTAINER_PREFIX="${ARTIFACT_LOWER}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Derive human-readable name from artifact if not provided
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -z "$NAME" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     NAME=""
| MCP:6Z97IX |     IFS='-' read -ra PARTS <<< "$ARTIFACT"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     for part in "${PARTS[@]}"; do
| MCP:6Z97IX |         NAME+="$(tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' <<< "${part:0:1}")${part:1} "
| MCP:6Z97IX |     done
| MCP:6Z97IX |     NAME="${NAME% }"
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Kafka group id
| MCP:6Z97IX | KAFKA_GROUP="${ARTIFACT}-group"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Print summary ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | info "Project initialization settings:"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Group ID:        $GROUP_ID"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Artifact:        $ARTIFACT"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Name:            $NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Author:          ${AUTHOR:-tzesh (unchanged)}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Package:         $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Package path:    $PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Core module:     $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  API module:      $API_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Container prefix: $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  PascalCase:      $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Step 1: Text replacements in file contents (before directory renames) ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | info "Replacing text in file contents..."
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Build list of files to process (exclude .git, binaries, target dirs)
| MCP:6Z97IX | FILES=$(find . -type f \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -path './.git/*' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -path '*/target/*' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -path './init-project.sh' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -path './.idea/*' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.jar' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.class' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.png' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.jpg' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.jpeg' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.gif' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.ico' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.svg' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.woff' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.woff2' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.ttf' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '*.eot' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name '.DS_Store' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name 'mvnw' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name 'mvnw.cmd' \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     -not -name 'maven-wrapper.jar' \
| MCP:6Z97IX | )
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Order matters: longer/more-specific strings first to avoid partial matches
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 1. Java package name (fully qualified)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com\.tzesh\.springtemplate|${PACKAGE_NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced com.tzesh.springtemplate -> $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 2. Java package path (slashes)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com/tzesh/springtemplate|${PACKAGE_PATH}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced com/tzesh/springtemplate -> $PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 3. Module names (longer first to avoid partial match)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template-core|${CORE_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template-api|${API_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template-api -> $API_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 4. Parent artifact name (after module-specific replacements)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-boot-template|${ARTIFACT}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring-boot-template -> $ARTIFACT"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 5. Kafka group id
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-template-group|${KAFKA_GROUP}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring-template-group -> $KAFKA_GROUP"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 6. Docker container names (underscored variants)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring_boot_template|${CONTAINER_PREFIX}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring_boot_template -> $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring_template|${CONTAINER_PREFIX}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring_template -> $CONTAINER_PREFIX"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 7. Core pom.xml <name> field (uses spring-template-core without "boot")
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|spring-template-core|${CORE_MODULE}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced spring-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 8. Human-readable names
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|Spring Boot Template|${NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced Spring Boot Template -> $NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|Spring Template|${NAME}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced Spring Template -> $NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 9. PascalCase (JWT issuer, GitHub repo name)
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|SpringBootTemplate|${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced SpringBootTemplate -> $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|SpringTemplate|${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced SpringTemplate -> $ARTIFACT_PASCAL"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 10. groupId in pom.xml and log4j2
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com\.tzesh|${GROUP_ID}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced com.tzesh -> $GROUP_ID"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|com/tzesh|${GROUP_ID//./\/}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced com/tzesh -> ${GROUP_ID//./\/}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 11. GitHub URL references
| MCP:6Z97IX | "${SED_I[@]}" "s|github\.com/tzesh/SpringBootTemplate|github.com/${ARTIFACT_PASCAL}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Replaced GitHub URL references"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # 12. Author tags (only if --author provided)
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -n "$AUTHOR" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     "${SED_I[@]}" "s|@author tzesh|@author ${AUTHOR}|g" $FILES 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX |     ok "Replaced @author tzesh -> @author $AUTHOR"
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Step 2: Rename Java package directories ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | info "Renaming Java package directories..."
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | OLD_PKG_PATH="com/tzesh/springtemplate"
| MCP:6Z97IX | NEW_PKG_PATH="$PACKAGE_PATH"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | for SOURCE_ROOT in \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     "spring-boot-template-core/src/main/java" \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     "spring-boot-template-core/src/test/java" \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     "spring-boot-template-api/src/main/java" \
| MCP:6Z97IX |     "spring-boot-template-api/src/test/java"; do
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX |     OLD_DIR="${SOURCE_ROOT}/${OLD_PKG_PATH}"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     NEW_DIR="${SOURCE_ROOT}/${NEW_PKG_PATH}"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX |     if [[ -d "$OLD_DIR" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |         mkdir -p "$NEW_DIR"
| MCP:6Z97IX |         # Move all contents (files and dirs) from old to new
| MCP:6Z97IX |         if ls -A "$OLD_DIR" | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |             cp -R "$OLD_DIR"/* "$NEW_DIR"/ 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX |             cp -R "$OLD_DIR"/.[!.]* "$NEW_DIR"/ 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX |             rm -rf "$OLD_DIR"
| MCP:6Z97IX |         fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX |         # Clean up empty parent directories
| MCP:6Z97IX |         OLD_PARENT="${SOURCE_ROOT}/com/tzesh"
| MCP:6Z97IX |         rmdir "$OLD_PARENT" 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX |         OLD_GRANDPARENT="${SOURCE_ROOT}/com"
| MCP:6Z97IX |         # Only remove 'com' if groupId doesn't start with 'com'
| MCP:6Z97IX |         if [[ "${GROUP_ID}" != com* ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |             rmdir "$OLD_GRANDPARENT" 2>/dev/null || true
| MCP:6Z97IX |         fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX |         ok "Moved $OLD_DIR -> $NEW_DIR"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     else
| MCP:6Z97IX |         warn "Directory not found: $OLD_DIR (skipped)"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | done
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Step 3: Rename module directories ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | info "Renaming module directories..."
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -d "spring-boot-template-core" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     mv "spring-boot-template-core" "$CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     ok "Renamed spring-boot-template-core -> $CORE_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | if [[ -d "spring-boot-template-api" ]]; then
| MCP:6Z97IX |     mv "spring-boot-template-api" "$API_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX |     ok "Renamed spring-boot-template-api -> $API_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | fi
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Step 4: Clean up ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | info "Cleaning up..."
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # Remove this script (self-delete)
| MCP:6Z97IX | SCRIPT_PATH="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)/$(basename "$0")"
| MCP:6Z97IX | rm -f "$SCRIPT_PATH"
| MCP:6Z97IX | ok "Removed init-project.sh"
| MCP:6Z97IX | 
| MCP:6Z97IX | # ── Done ──
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | printf "${GREEN}========================================${NC}\n"
| MCP:6Z97IX | printf "${GREEN}  Project initialized successfully!${NC}\n"
| MCP:6Z97IX | printf "${GREEN}========================================${NC}\n"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Project:   $NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Package:   $PACKAGE_NAME"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  Modules:   $CORE_MODULE, $API_MODULE"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "Next steps:"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  1. Review the changes: git diff"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  2. Build the project:  mvn clean compile"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo "  3. Run the tests:      mvn test"
| MCP:6Z97IX | echo ""
| MCP:6Z97IX | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ba74aa8cadc5daf37bbe9c15ddd33b162b9fc681)

data.content:
Based on the repository contents, here's a summary of how to initialize a new project from this Spring Boot template and start the required infrastructure locally:

## **1. Initializing a New Project from the Template**

### **Step 1: Use the GitHub Template**
- Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub to create a new repository
- Clone your new repository:
  ```sh
  git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
  cd <your-repo>
  ```

### **Step 2: Run the Initialization Script**
Execute the initialization script with your project details:

**Option A: With all parameters:**
```sh
./init-project.sh --group-id com.yourcompany --artifact your-app-name --name "Your App Name" --author "Your Name"
```

**Option B: Interactive mode (prompts for required values):**
```sh
./init-project.sh
```

**Script Parameters:**
| Parameter | Description | Required |
|-----------|-------------|----------|
| `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g., `com.acme`) | Yes |
| `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g., `my-app`) | Yes |
| `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |

**What the script does:**
- Renames Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` → your package)
- Updates module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` → `<artifact>-api`)
- Updates Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
- Replaces `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
- Self-deletes after completion

### **Step 3: Verify the Setup**
```sh
./mvnw clean install
```

## **2. Starting Required Infrastructure Locally**

### **Prerequisites**
- **Java 25** and **Maven 3.8.7+** are required
- **Docker** and **Docker Compose** must be installed

### **Infrastructure Services**
The template includes these containerized services:
1. **PostgreSQL 16** - Database (port 5432)
2. **Redis 7** - Caching/rate limiting (port 6379)
3. **Kafka 7.5.0** - Message broker (port 9092)
4. **Zookeeper 7.5.0** - Kafka coordination (port 2181)

### **Start Infrastructure Only (Development)**
```sh
# Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka

# Run the application
./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
```

### **Start Everything with Docker**
```sh
docker compose up -d
```

### **Environment Configuration**
Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables:
- `.env` - Development environment
- `.env.dev` - Development environment
- `.env.test` - Test environment  
- `.env.qa` - QA environment
- `.env.prod` - Production environment

**Example for different environments:**
```sh
# Test environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d

# Production environment
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
```

### **Accessing the Application**
- **API**: [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html)
- **Actuator endpoints**:
  - Health: `/api/v1/actuator/health`
  - Info: `/api/v1/actuator/info`
  - Metrics: `/api/v1/actuator/metrics`

### **Default Credentials (from `.env`)**
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with credentials `development`/`T3st!ng` and database `spring_template_dev`
- **Application**: Spring profile `dev` is active by default

### **Verification Commands**
After starting, you can verify everything is running:
```sh
# Check running containers
docker compose ps

# Check application health
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/actuator/health

# Check API documentation
# Open in browser: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html
```

**Note:** The initialization script modifies all configuration files to match your project name, so after running it, the container names, database names, and application references will be updated to your chosen artifact name.
